title: Singapore Primary Science question-section detection output (v1.0)
description: JSON shape emitted by Cursor agent science-question-section-detector.md (spec version v1.0). Covers the 2 canonical Science question types: MCQ, OEQ. Like the Math schema, there are no `stem_page_range`, `answers_in_separate_booklet`, or `answers_page_range` fields — science questions are self-contained and answers are always written in the same booklet as the questions. Each section carries a `question_info` array (one element per question index) with per-question mark and optional topic. See agent doc and science_exam_question_types.md for semantics.

schema_version.description: Must be 'science-v1.0' for Science question-section detection artifacts.

sections.description: Detected sections in document reading order.

uuidOrEmpty.description: `pdf_files.id` from Ai Study Buddy PdfFileManager (UUID); empty string when unset or lookup miss.

path.description: Canonical absolute path (`PdfFile.path`) from `PdfFileManager.get_file_by_path` after normalizing inputs, or from `get_file(file_id)`. Use "" only when unavailable.

role.description: How this file was used. Science papers always have answers in the same booklet as questions — use 'question_booklet' for a single standalone booklet, 'merged_pdf' when Booklet A and Booklet B (and the OAS sheet) appear concatenated in one file, 'unknown' when ambiguous.

notes.description: Per-file caveats (scan quality, encrypted, booklet merge order, lookup miss, etc.).

files.description: Input PDF paths the detector relied on.

hints.description: Parent-supplied hints (expected page ranges, OCR snippets, filename keywords, etc.).

notes.description: Detector-side input summary (which inputs were missing, render settings, booklet merge notes, etc.).

generation_model.description: Actual detector model id (never the YAML orchestration sentinel 'inherit').

confidence.description: Aggregate confidence for the entire detection run.

notes.description: Run-level commentary (merged PDF quirks, unrecognised layout, render failure, WA vs exam ambiguity, school-specific variant, etc.).

matched_header_text.description: Verbatim printed section or booklet header text that most strongly identified this section (e.g. 'BOOKLET A', 'BOOKLET B'). Use "" if none found.

matched_instruction_text.description: Verbatim printed section instruction that most strongly typed this section (e.g. 'For each question from 1 to 30, four options are given...(30 x 2 marks = 60 marks)'). Use "" if none found.

notes.description: Section-specific caveats (boundary ambiguity, atypical layout, WA vs exam format, question count variant, etc.). Use "" when none.

questionType.description: Canonical question type. MCQ: multiple-choice with OAS shading (Booklet A only in PSLE/standard format); all items carry 2 marks each — no mark-band split. OEQ: open-ended, answers written in booklet, [n] bracket printed on every answer line or sub-part (Booklet B only in PSLE/standard format), 2–5 marks per numbered question.

question_index.description: Printed question identifier at finest granularity: top-level ('Q1', 'Q31') when the question is not subdivided, sub-part ('Q31a', 'Q35c') when the paper explicitly labels sub-parts (a), (b), (c), etc. with separate answer lines. Always prefixed with uppercase Q.

question_mark.description: Marks allocated to this question index. For MCQ: always 2 (uniform band). For OEQ: read directly from the printed [n] bracket on the answer line or sub-part.

start_page.description: 1-based page index (within the inspected PDF) where this question's printed stimulus first appears. Always required — the detector has all pages rendered and can always determine this.

end_page.description: 1-based page index where this question's content (stimulus + answer space) ends. Include only when the question visibly spans more than one page (e.g. a long scenario with a large diagram continued onto the next page). Omit when the question fits on a single page.

question_topic.description: Free-form short description of what the question is about (the detector's best-effort reading of the question content). Keep under 30 words. Examples: 'life cycle of insect — identify stage where X does not eat', 'transport in plants — explain transpiration via xylem and phloem', 'identify organ in circulatory system'. Omit or use "" when the question is illegible or the topic is genuinely unclear.

printed_section_title.description: Verbatim printed booklet or section title when it adds information beyond the canonical `question_type` label (e.g. 'Booklet A', 'BOOKLET B', 'Section B: Open-ended Questions'). Omit or use "" when the paper uses no section title or the title matches the canonical name.

section_total_marks.description: Total marks for this detected section. Include only when (i) the numeric total read from the paper is trustworthy and (ii) that total applies to this canonical section alone. For MCQ: include when the total is clearly stated or derivable (e.g. 30 questions × 2 = 60). Omit when uncertain, illegible, or when a printed total clearly bundles multiple question types.

questions_page_range.description: 1-based page range (within the inspected PDF) where the question stimuli for this section appear. For MCQ, covers the printed question items and their option lists; for OEQ, covers printed question text, diagrams, answer lines, and the section instruction page.

question_info.description: Per-question metadata in reading order. One element per printed question index (at finest granularity). The sequence of question_index values in this array serves as the canonical question index list for the section.
